**Acceptance Criteria**

Fail.

- REQ-01 is not met. `ItemKindConfig` still models every tuple member as optional/defaulted rather than requiring each `[item_kinds.*]` declaration to declare all six fields. See [config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/config.rs:279): `section`, `id_pattern`, `markers`, `link_namespaces`, `scope`, and `source_of_truth` are all `Option`. The new tests explicitly bless missing fields, including missing `source-of-truth`, at [config.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/config.rs:1352). That contradicts the hard criterion “Each kind in `[item_kinds]` declares all six fields.”
- REQ-03 is at risk / not satisfied as written. The issue says existing inline-triple `label-coverage` rules continue unchanged and references extra-key round-tripping, but `expand_kind_sugar` now rejects any table combining `kind` with `criteria-section`, `marker`, or `id-pattern` at [rules.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:1301), and removes `kind` from sugared tables at [rules.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:1343). That is not a pure round-trip-preserving additive change for an existing inline rule that already carried an inert `kind` key.
- REQ-02 is covered by tests in [graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:1709).
- REQ-04 appears met: typed `criteria-label-match` and `criteria-to-check` retain `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` at [rules.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:1128) and [rules.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:1147).
- REQ-05 appears met for live engine logic; concrete kind-name literals are confined to tests/docs.
- REQ-06 has targeted coverage at [graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:1769).
